pattern: Because the appended value sits at the end of the underlying list, it only ever travels down the right spine, so a single walk down right children finds where to splice it in.
hints: The new value was appended to the end of the original list, so it can only become a node along the rightmost path of the tree.
Walk down right children while each node's value is larger than the new value; the place to insert is where that stops.
When you reach a node smaller than the value (or fall off the spine), make a new node holding the value, hang the current subtree as its left child, and reattach it to the parent's right pointer.

edge_inputs.description: new value becomes the new root
new value inserts deep on the right spine
value splices in pushing a subtree to its left child
single node tree, smaller value goes right
single node tree, larger value becomes root
value larger than every node climbs to the top

stress.description: build a maximum tree from a random unique permutation then insert a fresh value
stress.note: values stay unique so the maximum-tree shape is well defined
